prose:
Fukui indices for nitrobenzene (electrophilic f+, nucleophilic f−, and dual descriptors) were computed via the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill.

Method provenance
Property: atomic Fukui functions f+, f−, f0 and the corresponding dual descriptor; identification of the most electrophilic (largest f+) and most nucleophilic (largest f−) sites.
Level of theory: DFT, tier standard ⇒ B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied gas-phase geometry at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz (no re-optimization).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet); cation (N−1) and anion (N+1) states treated as doublets.
Software/backend: PySCF (via chemkit fukui-reactivity workflow).
Convergence & integrity: SCF cycles for N, N±1 states all converged; integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true.  The following warnings were returned by chemkit verbatim:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Live log: the run was streamed in real-time to a file named fukui_<timestamp>.out in the working directory (available for inspection).

Key results
• Most electrophilic atom (largest f+): atom 4 (C), f+ = 0.4964178.
• Most nucleophilic atom (largest f−): atom 5 (C), f− = 0.1072882.
• Complete per-atom values are provided in the output JSON at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpj98_0mpd/live.json.

Headline numeric result (reported above as required by the interface): 0.4964178 (f+ on atom 4).

All results are computed; no experimental data are referenced.
